sample.title: Can Cuba’s Economy Survive Trump?
sample.description:
Donald Trump’s blockade of Cuba, condemned by international groups, has caused shortages of fuel, food and medicine. Under threat from the US, will Cuba’s authoritarian government bend to his will? And as Havana seeks to rebuild a shattered economy, will it turn away from socialism and toward capitalism?

Read more on Bloomberg.com:

Cuba’s Bold Free-Market Turn Fails to Convince US It’s Ready to Change
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-02/cuba-economic-crisis-trump-unconvinced-by-free-market-reforms?srnd=undefined

Watch more Weekly Docs: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLqq4LnWs3olXOhUOv4O6r-9OdWTQN83se

Everything you need to know about the big topics shaping our world, direct from Bloomberg's global newsroom.

sample.title: Why Amex And Chase Love Lounges
sample.description:
American Express and Chase increasingly invest in cardholder perks, like lounges, at events and experiences around the world. Both companies have raised the fees on their premium credit cards as they seek to attract affluent cardholders and increase spending.

sample.title: Why McDonald's is supersizing in China
sample.description:
China is a major source of McDonald’s growth and it plans to have 10,000 locations in mainland China by the end of 2028.

CNBC's Eunice Yoon explains how the chain is winning over Chinese consumers with both value and nostalgia.

Find the full report in related video.

sample.title: This New Medicare Pilot Program Offers GLP-1s For $50 A Month. Here’s What You Need To Know
sample.description:
Americans on Medicare can now purchase GLP-1s for just $50 a month thanks to a temporary pilot program called Bridge.  Patients who meet certain requirements, like having  BMI of over 35 or having obesity-related conditions, may not qualify. CNBC’s Annika Constantino has more.

Produced, Shot and Edited by: Natalie Rice
Reporting by: Annika Constantino
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi

sample.title: ICE Could Sign A $220M Contract With A Taser Maker That Trump Invested Millions In
sample.description:
In February 2026, President Trump invested up to $5 million in top U.S. taser-maker Axon Enterprise. Two weeks later, ICE announced that it was seeking a $220 million contract to more than quadruple its inventory. Experts say that Axon is the only company able to meet the requirement outlined by ICE. CNBC’s Luke Fountain breaks it down.

Reporter: Luke Fountain
Produced by: Macklin Fishman
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Shot by: Charlotte Morabito
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.title: Squawk Pod: President Donald J. Trump: America at 250 - 07/03/26 | Audio Only
sample.description:
President Donald J. Trump sits down with Joe Kernen for an exclusive, extended interview ahead of America’s 250th anniversary. In a wide-ranging conversation, the President discusses the stock market’s performance, the war in Iran, the Supreme Court’s latest decisions, AI innovation, and the government’s stake in Intel. Plus, President Trump addresses his own financial disclosures and considers his legacy in the context of the Presidents who came before him. Happy Fourth of July!

In this episode:
Joe Kernen, @JoeSquawk
President Donald J. Trump, @POTUS

For access to live and exclusive video from CNBC subscribe to CNBC PRO: https://cnb.cx/42d859g

sample.title: Pope Leo visits migrant hub Lampedusa on July 4
sample.description:
Pope Leo, the first U.S.-born leader of the Catholic Church, is spending Independence Day on the Italian island of Lampedusa, a main landing point for migrants crossing from North Africa to Europe. Leo, who has urged compassion for migrants, will visit one of the Mediterranean's deadliest migration routes, where many arrive in fishing boats and makeshift dinghies. 

#popeleo #america250 #independenceday #immigration #catholic

sample.title: Extreme heat could change the mix for European drinks makers
sample.description:
The European heatwave is testing the assumption that hot summers mean a boost in sales of alcohol, with studies showing drinkers less inclined to go for a chilled beer or Aperol ‌Spritz when it gets too hot, prompting companies to rethink their offerings.
 
#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #europeheatwave #alcoholsales #alcoholconsumption

sample.title: Should schools be more flexible on Monday after England face Mexico? #BBCNews
What it’s like to be a reporter in Trump’s White House | BBC News
sample.description:
In Donald Trump’s White House the news is nonstop. New developments are breaking day and night.
 
Follow Sarah Smith, the BBC’s North America Editor, as she follows every move in the West Wing and beyond, interpreting the significance and communicating that to the BBC’s global audience.
 
From the halls of Congress where representatives are investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files, to the streets of Washington where President Trump is remaking America’s capital, and into the White House briefing room with Vice-President JD Vance, get a glimpse beyond the headlines and discover how the BBC covers this presidency.
 
Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#Trump #USpolitics #WhiteHouse #BBCNews
